details: Package aikaf668897@1.0.3 poses as a string utility library but runs a reverse shell on install. The postinstall hook spawns a detached Node.js process executing scripts/shell.js, which opens a TCP reverse shell to 114[.]67[.]90[.]67:3333. On Linux it spawns /bin/sh -i and on Windows it spawns a hidden powershell.exe process. The shell automatically reconnects every 10 seconds on error or disconnect. The bundled index.js contains decoy string-manipulation functions (capitalize, truncate, camelCase, etc.) to conceal the payload. Package aikaf668897@1.0.3 is a string-utility library trojanized with a reverse shell. It exports legitimate string functions (capitalize, truncate, camelCase) from index.js as camouflage. However, its postinstall hook (scripts/postinstall.js) launches a detached child process running scripts/shell.js. shell.js opens a persistent TCP reverse shell to 114[.]67[.]90[.]67:3333 — on Unix it spawns /bin/sh -i; on Windows it spawns hidden powershell.exe. The shell's stdin/stdout/stderr are piped over the socket, giving the remote attacker interactive shell access. It auto-reconnects every 10 seconds on disconnect. Runtime testing confirmed outbound connections to 114[.]67[.]90[.]67:3333 with HTTP capture data exchanged.
